title: Trump shares video of massive explosions in Iran's Isfahan; bunker-busters used?
content: US President Donald Trump intensified his rhetoric on the military campaign against Iran, sharing a striking image of Isfahan ablaze following Operation Epic Fury. American forces reportedly used powerful bunker-buster bombs on a large ammunition depot in the city, which also houses facilities linked to Iran's nuclear program. This follows previous strikes targeting its nuclear infrastructure.

title: Iran strikes fully loaded Kuwaiti oil tanker at Dubai port; spill risk flagged
content: A Kuwaiti crude oil tanker anchored off Dubai was targeted in a direct Iranian attack, igniting a fire and damaging its hull. This incident, involving drone strikes, follows a pattern of attacks on commercial shipping in the Gulf. Dubai authorities are managing the blaze, with all 24 crew members reported safe.

title: Why Vaibhav Sooryavanshi said no to 'birthday cake' before smashing CSK
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi starred in Rajasthan Royals' IPL 2026 opener, smashing a rapid 52 off 17 balls against Chennai Super Kings.  He prioritized rest over birthday celebrations to deliver a match-winning powerplay performance, guiding his team to an emphatic eight-wicket victory.  The young sensation credited his coaches and opening partner for his confident display.

title: Stock market holiday today: Are BSE, NSE closed for trading on March 31, 2026?
content: This holiday is the fifth among the 16 market closures planned for 2026. Trading will remain suspended again on April 3 (Friday) for Good Friday, followed by another holiday on April 14 (Tuesday) to mark Dr. B.R. Ambedkar Jayanti.

title: Starting April 1, USCIS alert says that the immigration authority will reject forms
content: USCIS will only accept the 02/27/26 edition of Form I-129 for H-1B and other visa applications starting April 1, 2026. The updated form requires employers to detail educational requirements and experience, aligning with the Department of Labor's wage level assignments for the new wage-weighted lottery system.

title: ‘Dhurandhar: The Revenge’ inches close to Rs 1,400 crore worldwide in 12 days
content: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' has achieved massive box office success, becoming his highest-grossing film ever. The spy thriller sequel surpassed its predecessor's records, earning over Rs 800 crore net in India and Rs 1300 crore gross worldwide. Despite a weekday dip, the film's strong performance continues to impress.

title: Pentagon rejects report that Hegseth attempted defence investments before Iran attack
content: A report alleged a broker explored defense investments before US-Israeli strikes on Iran. Sean Parnell, assistant to US War Secretary Pete Hegseth, has vehemently denied these claims. He stated the allegations are false and fabricated. Parnell demanded an immediate retraction of the report. Secretary Hegseth and the Department of War maintain their commitment to ethics and regulations.

title: Microsoft AI CEO reveals what will really decide AI's winners and losers by 2028
content: Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman asserts that the AI industry's future hinges on who can afford to run models at scale, not just who builds the smartest ones.  He argues that inference compute scarcity will define winners for the next few years, with high-margin products gaining a significant edge through a data-driven improvement flywheel.

title: PSL controversy: Afridi fined for 'forcefully escorting' 4 visitors to hotel room
content: Pakistan pacer Shaheen Afridi has been fined PKR 1 million by Lahore Qalandars for breaching hotel security protocols during the PSL. He and teammate Sikandar Raza reportedly hosted guests in Raza's room late at night, despite explicit refusal from authorities. The franchise acknowledged the incident, citing a misunderstanding.

title: 'Agastya, are you ok?' Hardik Pandya's girlfriend Mahieka snaps at photographers
content: Hardik Pandya's girlfriend, Mahieka Sharma, protected cricketer's son Agastya from intrusive photographers after a Mumbai Indians match.  She shielded the child from camera flashes, visibly annoyed by the crowd's behavior. Meanwhile, Mumbai Indians secured a thrilling six-wicket victory against Kolkata Knight Riders, marking their first season-opening win since 2012.

title: Vizag: Navy staffer kills lover, stores torso in fridge, burns limbs in hills
content: In a shocking turn of events, a married officer from the Indian Navy in Visakhapatnam faces arrest for the brutal murder of a woman. Authorities allege he dismembered her body, hiding some parts in his residence and incinerating others. The victim's father refutes the man's assertions of financial motives, claiming the crime was an attempt to safeguard his professional reputation.

title: Elon Musk provides 'Big' update on Tesla investment in Japan
content: Tesla is making a major investment in Japan. The electric vehicle maker is expanding its service centers and Supercharger network. This move aims to support increasing demand for Tesla cars. The company plans to more than double its service locations this year. Tesla also highlighted its strong industrial ties with Japan.

title: Oil prices today: Crude jumps after war disrupts supply routes, global shortage fear looms
content: Oil prices have jumped significantly. Brent crude is over $110 and WTI is above $100. Supply worries are growing due to the conflict in the Middle East. Iran's actions and Houthi missile strikes are impacting key shipping routes. This situation threatens global energy flows. Saudi Arabia is rerouting its oil. The US has warned Iran about its energy infrastructure.

title: Celtics vs Hawks injury report: Who's playing, injured and questionable players
content: Boston Celtics, on a three-game win streak, face the surging Atlanta Hawks in a crucial playoff seeding clash. Both teams grapple with key player injuries, making team depth and rotation pivotal. Boston's Jayson Tatum, Jaylen Brown, and Derrick White are game-time decisions, while Atlanta monitors Dyson Daniels, Onyeka Okongwu, and Jonathan Kuminga.

title: TJ Watt’s $123M deal criticized as Aaron Rodgers eyes new contract
content: The Pittsburgh Steelers face a significant financial challenge in the 2026 offseason. With T.J. Watt's hefty contract and Aaron Rodgers seeking a substantial raise, the Steelers' cap space is severely strained. Watt's production has declined, raising questions about his value, while Rodgers' strong performance has inflated his market, forcing a tough decision on which star to prioritise. Now, they must choose. Stick with a high-priced defence led by Watt or adjust the books to bring Rodgers back. Either way, the margin for error is gone.

title: MLB trade rumors: San Diego Padres eye $38.5M Boston Red Sox Star to fix rotation
content: The San Diego Padres are eyeing Lucas Giolito for their 2026 baseball season rotation. With Yu Darvish's salary off the books, the team has funds to acquire a reliable pitcher. Giolito offers stability to a rotation needing depth. A potential two-year deal provides flexibility for both the Padres and Giolito.

title: Trump eyes Arab nations to fund Iran war amid peace talks with Tehran
content: Speaking at a press briefing, White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said the proposal was something Trump had been considering, though she stopped short of confirming any formal move. Earlier, Trump issued a stark warning to Iran, saying its energy infrastructure, including oil facilities and desalination plants, could face widespread destruction if Tehran does not move quickly toward a deal.

title: 'Ramayana': 'Rama' first glimpse rating and runtime revealed
content: Nitesh Tiwari's 'Ramayana', featuring Ranbir Kapoor, has received a 'U' rating from the CBFC for its teaser, 'Rama', set for release on April 2. This trailer-length clip, running 2 minutes and 38 seconds, follows earlier glimpses of the epic.  The film, with a star-studded cast, is slated for a grand world reveal, promising a visually stunning presentation.

title: Explosive Vaibhav Sooryavanshi creates history for Rajasthan Royals
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi unleashed a stunning 52 off 17 balls, including five sixes, to power Rajasthan Royals to an emphatic eight-wicket victory over Chennai Super Kings in their IPL 2026 opener. His blistering knock, the third-fastest IPL half-century, set up a dominant chase, marking RR's biggest win by balls remaining.

title: CSK Jamie Overton surpasses MS Dhoni, sets new record in defeat vs RR
content: Despite Chennai Super Kings' dismal IPL 2026 opener, Jamie Overton delivered a record-breaking performance. Walking in at 82/8, Overton scored 43 off 36 balls, the highest score by a CSK batter at No. 8 or below, surpassing MS Dhoni's previous record. His defiant knock offered a rare highlight in a match dominated by Rajasthan Royals.

title: 'Gender identity a right': Rajasthan high court tears into transgender bill
content: The Rajasthan High Court has made headlines by branding the state’s transgender reservation policy as nothing but a 'superficial mask'. This decision came with a mandate granting an additional 3% weight to transgender applicants in both education and employment.

title: Mahavir Jayanti: Best wishes, greetings to share with family and friends
content: article offers 50 curated wishes, messages, and quotes for family, friends, and social media. These messages focus on peace, compassion, and truth. They aim to convey genuine sentiment and spiritual depth for Mahavir Jayanti 2026.

title: No IPL telecast in Bangladesh as JioStar terminates broadcast agreement
content: The development comes at a time when Bangladesh authorities were reviewing a ban on IPL telecast. With the deal now terminated, there is no immediate option for the tournament to return to television screens in the country.

title: Divya fails to exploit Vaishali’s inaccuracy to miss opportunity for Candidates lead
content: While all the four games in the women’s section were drawn for the second consecutive day, there was some drama in the Divya-Vaishali clash. Divya missed Qg4 on the 37th turn which would have made Vaishali rue her 34th knight-move that had tilted the computer evaluation from 0.15 to 2.47 in Divya’s favour. It was not clearly winning but it would have troubled Vaishali a lot.

title: Atanu Chakraborty rebuts HDFC MD, flags ‘deeper governance issues’ behind exit
content: Former HDFC Bank chairman Atanu Chakraborty disputes MD Sashidhar Jagdishan's claims regarding staff action on high-risk bond sales. Chakraborty asserts his resignation stemmed from a larger governance issue and a lack of alignment on ethics and values, not personality clashes or Jagdishan's reappointment. He emphasized the need for proactive risk avoidance.

title: Israel passes law allowing hanging for Palestinian convicted of deadly terror acts
content: The measure marks a significant win for the country’s far-right bloc and reflects a tougher posture toward Palestinians following the Hamas-led October 7, 2023 attack and the subsequent war in Gaza. While capital punishment has long existed in Israeli law, it has been carried out only twice in the country’s 78-year history.

title: Mohsin Naqvi 'unhappy' as PSL hit by hotel-security row, ball-tampering controversy
content: The situation comes at a time when PSL 2026 is already being conducted under restrictions. Matches in Lahore are being held without spectators, and the tournament is running with fewer venues due to fuel and travel issues in Pakistan linked to the West Asia crisis. The PCB had put measures in place to manage the event, but the league has seen multiple controversies in quick succession.

title: Bulls waive guard Ivey after anti-gay comments
content: The Chicago Bulls waived guard Jaden Ivey after his recent anti-gay comments amid several rants on religion and other topics.

title: Wemby records fastest double-double ever in win
content: Victor Wembanyama had a season-high 41 points and 16 rebounds while collecting the fastest double-double in NBA history in the Spurs' win Monday.

title: Texas returns to Final Four with rout of Michigan
content: No. 1 seed Texas continued what has been a drama-free NCAA tournament run with a 77-41 victory over 2-seed Michigan on Monday to reach the Final Four for the second year in a row.

title: 49ers HC, owner deliver mixed signals on Aiyuk
content: 49ers coach Kyle Shanahan and owner Jed York delivered differing messages on Brandon Aiyuk's future Monday.

title: Ravens blame 'slow news cycle' for Crosby fallout
content: Ravens team president Sashi Brown said Monday that the rescinding of the Maxx Crosby trade won't negatively affect the team's reputation, chalking up the criticism to "a slow news cycle."

title: Haslam: Watson has 'great chance' to be QB1
content: One year after calling the trade for Deshaun Watson a "big swing-and-miss," Browns owner Jimmy Haslam said the quarterback has a "fresh start" with new coach Todd Monken.

content: The defending Super Bowl champion Seahawks will be featured on this summer's "Hard Knocks," with the Patriots taking center stage in summer 2027.

content: After Texas and South Carolina's wins, the Final Four is set! Here's everything you need to know about Monday's action.

content: Igor Tudor will be remembered at Tottenham Hotspur as the firefighter who used gasoline instead of water. That's if he's remembered at all.

content: Max Verstappen predicted F1's new rule set would underwhelm, and 2026 is proving him correct, so much so that he might just quit the sport entirely.

content: Renovations to Lucas Oil Stadium will be costly. Irsay-Gordon acknowledged diversified funding could help.

content: According to Jokic, the explanation behind Clark's high praise for him Sunday night is simple: The two share an agent.

title: Delimitation row: Revanth Reddy wants current gap in seats be maintained, as 50% increase in Lok Sabha seats will leave South at a disadvantage
content: If the 50% model is applied uniformly, the five southern States collectively would gain aroud 66 seats, while Hindi-belt and northern States, would see a rise of roughly 142 seats, Mr. Reddy said

title: Statues come alive as poll season warms up in U.P.
content: Multiple incidents involving statues of historical figures spark protests and political mobilisation among caste groups in western Uttar Pradesh ahead of 2027 poll despite a ban on caste markers

title: Former civil servants oppose ‘illegal’ deployment of Assam forest force on poll duty
content: Signatories say poll body’s norms do not allow deployment of forest staff for election work; these safeguards ensure that forest protection and wildlife management functions are not disrupted

title: BJP puts up united front as Suvendu Adhikari files nomination for Nandigram
content: Once sidelined BJP leader Dilip Ghosh was seen alongside Suvendu as he went to file his nomination for the high stakes West Bengal Assembly elections

title: CM Siddaramaiah kicks off bypoll campaign at Bagalkot terming Centre ‘anti-people’ for not notifying tribunal verdict
content: Mr. Siddaramaiah said even when the BJP was in power in the State for four years, no compensation was given to farmers

title: 1.22 lakh Anganwadi workers in Rajasthan get ₹1,000 each through DBT
content: The Chief Minister said the children aged three to six years were being provided hot milk five days a week at Anganwadi centres under the Amrit Aahar Yojana. “These buildings are being renovated, and smartphones have been distributed to workers and women supervisors”

title: J&K Assembly starts debate on 33 private members Bills, three fail to pass
content: “J&K’s successive governments, including the present dispensation led by Omar Abdullah, have ensured protection of temples, mosques, gurdwaras and churches alike,” said Deputy Chief Minister Surinder Choudhary, while opposing the Bill of the BJP

title: Transgender Amendment Bill gets President Murmu’s assent
content: A day after the Rajya Sabha passed the Bill, around 140 lawyers and feminists wrote to Ms. Murmu, urging her not to grant assent to the Bill, pointing out “constitutional violations” in its provisions and “procedural infirmities” in the way it was passed

title: BJP fields Harshad Parmar for Umreth bypoll in Gujarat
content: The bypoll has been necessitated following the death ofGovind Parmar, who passed away on March 6 at the age of 72 after a brief illness. He had represented the Umreth seat in Anand distric

title: JKPC chief Sajad Lone highlights official data mismatch on poor population in J&K’s regions, seeks one definition
content: Speaking in the J&K Assembly, Mr. Lone said official data contradicted its own figures on how many poor families live in the two regions of Kashmir and Jammu and termed it as “a structural flaw that is distorting fairness in employment and welfare distribution”

title: Putting trans people under OBC quota gives no real benefit, says Rajasthan High Court
content: The Rajasthan High Court also criticised the Centre’s amendment Bill taking away the right to gender self-determination as something that risks reducing an ‘inviolable aspect of personhood’ to a ‘contingent, State-mediated entitlement’

title: Amidst West Asia crisis, India purchased natural gas at  $19/unit from spot markets for fertiliser units
content: According to govt officials, there has been a “sharp increase” in price of inputs as LNG, ammonia and sulphur alongside a spike in freight and logistics cost.

title: PACL case: ED facilitates restitution of 455 properties worth ₹15,582 crore to Justice Lodha Committee
content: During the current financial year, the agency has attached assets worth about ₹26,324 Crore, taking the total attachment in the case so far to ₹27,030 crore. They include properties located both in India and Australia

title: UDF began preparations for Mission 2026 a year ago; it will win in Kerala: Satheesan
content: Kerala’s Leader of Opposition V.D. Satheesan says the Congress has brought back 90% of the social groups that left the UDF; he says the LDF government has put the State in a “huge debt trap” and has “failed in the health, education, agriculture and welfare sectors” due to which there is a current against the government

title: BJP seeks action against Mamata over ‘MCC breach’
content: A delegation comprising Union Ministers Piyush Goyal, Kiren Rijiju, and Sukanta Majumdar among others submitted the complaint to the Election Commission; the party accused Ms. Banerjee of making ‘grave and disturbing public statements’ during multiple public rallies in Bengal, ‘posing a serious threat to free, fair and peaceful elections’

title: The political does not get personal in campaign in parts of Assam
content: Jorhat, Tamulpur, and two Guwahati constituencies are among the few where candidates are demonstrating mutual respect rather than bitterness raging elsewhere

title: Delhi Police arrest Lashkar-e-Taiba operative
content: The arrest of Shabir Ahmed Lone, according to the police, came after they learnt about his connections with the “anti-India” posters in several Delhi metro stations on February 22.

content: The peripheral ring road will be 23 km in length, of which only a 19.80 km stretch has been taken up for construction in package-1

content: Consumers get water supply once a week, forcing them to walk for a few kilometres to fetch a few pots of water for domestic use

title: Scattered rainfall likely in parts of Tamil Nadu; heat to remain under check: Regional Meteorological Centre
content: The Regional Meteorological Centre (RMC) has forecast light to moderate rainfall in Western Ghats and adjoining districts and south coastal Tamil Nadu

title: Barring one, entire top leadership of Maoists wiped out, Amit Shah tells Lok Sabha
content: In the past three years, 706 Maoists were killed, 2,218 were arrested, and over 4,800 cadres surrendered

title: Centre eyes new regulation to cover social media users
content: Govt. may permit I&B Ministry to send takedown notices to individual users for their posts; It includes controversial clause in draft amendment to IT Rules 2021; Internet Freedom Foundation calls it ‘massive expansion of unconstitutional censorship’

content: Principal District and Sessions Judge G. Prabhavati, who is also the chairperson of Chamarajanagar District Legal Services Authority, says the cases included 2,950 court and 82,957 pre-litigation cases.

title: Trump To Call On Arab Nations To Pay For Iran War? What White House Said

content: Trump has repeatedly claimed to be making diplomatic progress - though Tehran denies negotiating directly - while ramping up his threats and sending thousands more US troops to the Middle East.

content: US Israel Iran War News LIVE Updates: The United States and Israel continue to strike Iran, whose retaliatory attacks have targeted Israel and neighboring Gulf Arab states. More than 3,000 people have been killed.

content: US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said on Monday in an interview with Fox News that the United States is going to retake control of the Strait of Hormuz.

content: A man who crashed his pickup truck into a Detroit-area synagogue earlier in March was carrying out an attack inspired by Hezbollah, the Iran-backed terrorist organization, the FBI said Monday.

content: The Supreme Court reaffirmed the right to die with dignity under Article 21, allowing withdrawal of life support and emphasising patient autonomy; it shows a shift towards dignity and relief from suffering, while raising concerns about misuse and social inequality

title: Congress slams attack on its candidate in Assam election
content: The convoy of Sunil Kumar Chhetri, the party’s nominee from Naduar seat, was attacked by unidentified men around 3 p.m. when he was travelling for an election campaign; he fell during a scuffle and has been taken to a hospital for treatment

title: Energy imports from Africa are steadily on the rise as India diversifies to plug Hormuz gap
content: . Atul Mathur is a Senior Assistant Editor at The Times of India with over 27 years of experience in journalism. Based in Delhi, he has spent much of his career reporting on governance, public policy and politics, churning out researched, data-driven stories that impact daily lives. Atul is known for investigative depth and strong human-interest narratives as he strives to bring clarity and context to complex issues. He currently tracks the energy sector, writing on power, renewable energy, coal and mines. Read More

title: 10 India-bound ships carrying energy cargo stuck in Persian Gulf
content: 10 foreign-flagged ships carrying energy products are stranded in the Persian Gulf . this is in addition to 18 Indian vessels currently in the region . three of the ships are loaded with LPG, four are carrying crude oil . two LPG carriers cleared the Strait of Hormuz on Saturday .

title: Indian killed in Kuwait desalination plant attack
content:
.

NEW DELHI: An Indian national was killed in an attack on a power and water desalination plant in Kuwait, taking the toll of Indians killed in the ongoing war in West Asia to eight. “Embassy of India in Kuwait expresses its condolences at the tragic demise of an Indian national due to an attack on a desalination facility in Kuwait yesterday. The embassy is coordinating with Kuwaiti authorities to render all possible support and assistance,” said the Indian embassy on X, confirming the first Indian death in Kuwait in the conflict. Kuwait’s ministry of electricity, water and renewable energy said a service building at one of its power and water distillation plants was damaged during the attack, according to the state-run KUNA news agency. Officials described it as a “brutal attack”. The plant where the attack occurred wasn’t identified. Ministry spokesperson Fatima Jawhar Hayat said the strike resulted in the death of a worker of Indian nationality, Gulf News reported.

End of Article

title: Design in India or lose subsidy, IT & electronic min Ashwini Vaishnaw tells electronics companies
content: . Manash Pratim Gohain is a seasoned journalist with over two decades at The Times of India, where he has built a rich body of work spanning education policy, politics, and governance. Renowned for his incisive coverage of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, accreditation reforms, and skilling initiatives, he has also reported on student politics, urban policy, and social movements. His political reportage—both reflective and news-driven—adds depth to his writing, bridging policy with public impact. Through his 2,500 articles and related outlets, he has emerged as a trusted voice in national discourse, particularly in linking education reform to broader societal change. Read More

title: Congress seeks West Asia debate; already briefed in detail, says govt
content: parliamentary affairs minister Kiren Rijiju says extensive statements have been made on the issue in parliament . the demand for the debate was raised after Question Hour . he said the government is ready to talk with the protesting members . opposition MPs persisted with their demand .

title: India sends 7,000 tonnes diesel to Bangladesh
content:
.

NEW DELHI: Bangladesh is receiving a fresh consignment of 7,000 tonnes of diesel through a pipeline from India’s Numaligarh refinery, with energy minister Iqbal Hasan Mahmud Tuku Monday telling Parliament that fuel hoarding — not a supply shortage amid the West Asia crisis — is the bigger concern. The fuel began arriving through the India-Bangladesh Friendship Pipeline Saturday evening and is expected to be fully delivered by Tuesday. Earlier, 15,000 tonnes were brought via the pipeline, after a 5,000-tonne shipment from March 25, to meet the needs of Bangladesh, heavily dependent on Gulf imports. Officials said Bangladesh Petroleum Corporation is prioritising pipeline imports alongside sea routes, with supply steady.

End of Article

title: Big boost for maritime strength: Navy gets delivery of 2 warships, 1 survey vessel in one day
content: the Indian Navy received deliveries of two warships, including a stealth frigate, and one large survey ship from Garden Reach Shipbuilding and Engineers Ltd (GRSE) in just one day . Dunagiri is the fifth warship of Nilgiri class made under the Project 17A and delivered to the Navy in the last 16 months .
